questions.question: A man covers a distance of 90 km in 8 hours partly on foot at 3 km/h and partly on bicycle at 15 km/h. What is the distance covered on foot?
questions.explanation: Let foot distance be x km. Time equation: x/3 + (90-x)/15 = 8. Solving gives x = 15 km.

questions.question: Two trains of lengths 150 m and 250 m run on parallel tracks at 45 km/h and 63 km/h respectively. How long will they take to cross each other if running in opposite directions?
questions.explanation: Relative speed = 45+63 = 108 km/h = 30 m/s. Total length = 400 m. Time = 400/30 ≈ 13.33 sec, closest option is 14.3 sec.

questions.question: Two trains of lengths 180 m and 220 m run on parallel tracks at 60 km/h and 84 km/h respectively. How long will they take to cross each other if running in opposite directions?
questions.explanation: Relative speed = 60+84 = 144 km/h = 40 m/s. Total length = 400 m. Time = 400/40 = 10 sec.

questions.question: Two trains of lengths 250 m and 350 m run on parallel tracks at 36 km/h and 54 km/h respectively. How long will they take to cross each other if running in opposite directions?
questions.explanation: Relative speed = 36+54 = 90 km/h = 25 m/s. Total length = 600 m. Time = 600/25 = 24 sec.

A thief is spotted by a policeman 300 m ahead. The thief runs at 8 km/h and the policeman at 11 km/h. How far must the policeman run to catch the thief?
questions.explanation: Relative speed = 3 km/h. Time to catch = 0.3/3 = 0.1 h. Distance policeman runs = 11 * 0.1 = 1.1 km.

questions.question: A thief is spotted by a policeman 150 m ahead. The thief runs at 9 km/h and the policeman at 13 km/h. How far must the policeman run to catch the thief?
questions.explanation: Relative speed = 4 km/h. Time = 0.15/4 = 0.0375 h. Distance = 13 * 0.0375 = 0.4875 km.

questions.question: A thief is spotted by a policeman 250 m ahead. The thief runs at 6 km/h and the policeman at 10 km/h. How far must the policeman run to catch the thief?
questions.explanation: Relative speed = 4 km/h. Time = 0.25/4 = 0.0625 h. Distance = 10 * 0.0625 = 0.625 km.

questions.question: A boat travels 30 km upstream in 5 hours and 48 km downstream in 4 hours. What is the speed of the stream?
questions.explanation: Upstream speed = 6 km/h, downstream speed = 12 km/h. Stream speed = (12-6)/2 = 3 km/h.

questions.question: A boat travels 20 km upstream in 4 hours and 45 km downstream in 3 hours. What is the speed of the stream?
questions.explanation: Upstream speed = 5 km/h, downstream speed = 15 km/h. Stream speed = (15-5)/2 = 5 km/h.

questions.question: A boat travels 18 km upstream in 3 hours and 40 km downstream in 5 hours. What is the speed of the stream?
questions.explanation: Upstream speed = 6 km/h, downstream speed = 8 km/h. Stream speed = (8-6)/2 = 1 km/h.

questions.question: A train 200 m long crosses a platform 100 m long in 15 seconds. What is the speed of the train?
questions.explanation: Total distance = 200 + 100 = 300 m. Speed = 300/15 = 20 m/s = 72 km/h.

questions.question: A train 400 m long crosses a platform 250 m long in 30 seconds. What is the speed of the train?
questions.explanation: Total distance = 400 + 250 = 650 m. Speed = 650/30 = 21.67 m/s ≈ 78 km/h.

questions.question: A person travels 40 km at 40 km/h and another 40 km at 80 km/h. What is the average speed for the entire journey?
questions.explanation: Total distance = 80 km. Time = 40/40 + 40/80 = 1 + 0.5 = 1.5 h. Average speed = 80/1.5 = 53.33 km/h.

questions.question: A person travels 60 km at 30 km/h and another 60 km at 90 km/h. What is the average speed for the entire journey?
questions.explanation: Total distance = 120 km. Time = 60/30 + 60/90 = 2 + 0.6667 = 2.6667 h. Average speed = 120/2.6667 = 45 km/h.

questions.question: A person travels 50 km at 25 km/h and another 50 km at 75 km/h. What is the average speed for the entire journey?
questions.explanation: Total distance = 100 km. Time = 50/25 + 50/75 = 2 + 0.6667 = 2.6667 h. Average speed = 100/2.6667 = 37.5 km/h.
